title: bohadschia alliacea
intro: Bohadschia alliacea, also known as Muco de Jamaica or Muco, is a plant species native to the tropical regions of Central and South America, belonging to the family Asclepiadaceae. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its ability to treat respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Bohadschia alliacea include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate digestive issues, and support immune function. Bohadschia alliacea can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or decoction, and its mucilaginous properties also make it a popular ingredient in traditional herbal remedies, such as cough syrups and expectorants.
